summary: Goal contract was set from the 2026-05-19 external comparison report; branch/runtime reconciliation is closed for 30m trial execution, while promotion remains blocked by learning review and remediation backlog evidence.

observations.surface: goal runtime contract, worktree guard, auto-improve readiness, branch-only safety guard files
observations.problem: The external report shows that the current workspace is a dirty main checkout with zip-derived/local runtime state, while the canonical goal branch adds fixed-point, transient cleanup, clean fixture regeneration, and workflow helper guard files that are absent here.
observations.why_it_matters: A 5-day goal runtime launched from this state could produce plausible-looking artifacts without the branch safety checks that prove the artifacts agree, stale transients are cleaned, fixture regeneration is not contaminated, and promotion remains blocked while evidence is dirty.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a narrow reconciliation target that checks out or verifies the canonical goal branch, asserts the branch-only guard file set exists, refreshes goal worktree guard and readiness, and refuses long-run execution until the fixed-point, cleanup, and fixture guards pass.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed in this task: local main now contains the goal runtime guard set, the checked-in goal/readiness snapshots are refreshed, goal-runtime fixed-point and cleanup guards pass, and readiness has one runnable proposal for a bounded 30m trial. Keep promotion blocked until the learning review and remediation backlog are cleared.

observations.surface: Makefile goal runtime targets and local Python interpreter selection
observations.problem: The repo default python executable lacks jsonschema/PyYAML in this workspace, while .venv/bin/python has the required dependencies and successfully runs the goal client tests.
observations.why_it_matters: Goal preflight and set_goal operations can fail before producing evidence if an operator uses the ambient python instead of the repo dependency environment.
observations.automation_candidate: Teach the goal runtime preflight or Make defaults to prefer the repo venv when present, or emit a targeted dependency diagnostic before invoking schema-backed goal scripts.
observations.suggested_followup: Keep using .venv/bin/python for this workspace until the broader Python/toolchain contract is reconciled; consider a small guard that prints the chosen interpreter and dependency availability before long-run start.

observations.surface: make auto-improve-readiness, refresh-generated-core, ops/manifest.json, goal worktree guard
observations.problem: The full auto-improve-readiness target refreshes core generated artifacts before the readiness body, and in a full-vault checkout this rewrites ops/manifest.json plus runtime reports before the embedded worktree guard runs.
observations.why_it_matters: The target can create its own dirty worktree evidence and then report promotion blocked by git_worktree_dirty even when the operator started from a clean checkout; committing the refreshed manifest can also risk carrying local-only inventory into a public-safe surface.
observations.automation_candidate: Split auto-improve-readiness into a preflight-safe mode that writes refresh-generated-core outputs to tmp or runs the worktree guard before tracked refreshes, then only promotes canonical reports after public/private boundary checks pass.
observations.suggested_followup: Keep using auto-improve-readiness-report-body for canonical readiness refresh after curated core evidence is committed or restored; add a small regression test that full auto-improve-readiness cannot self-introduce a dirty worktree blocker through ops/manifest.json.
